questions.question: A painter mixes $2 \cdot \sqrt{3}$ liters of blue paint with $5 \cdot \sqrt{2}$ liters of white paint. What is the ratio of blue paint to total paint, with a rationalized denominator?
A musician plays a rhythm with a frequency of $\frac{1}{\sqrt{5}}$ Hz. What is this frequency with a rationalized denominator?
The area of a rectangular canvas is $4 \cdot \sqrt{3}$ square meters. If its length is $2 \cdot \sqrt{2}$ meters, what is its width with a rationalized denominator?
A sound wave has a frequency of $\frac{1}{2 \cdot \sqrt{7}}$ Hz. What is this frequency with a rationalized denominator?
A painter needs $\frac{3}{\sqrt{11}}$ liters of paint to cover a wall. What is the amount of paint needed with a rationalized denominator?
The length of a string is $5 \cdot \sqrt{2}$ meters. If it is divided into $\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}$ parts, what is the length of each part with a rationalized denominator?
A musician plays a song with a rhythm pattern of $\frac{2}{\sqrt{5}}$ beats per minute. What is this rhythm pattern with a rationalized denominator?
The area of a triangular canvas is $6 \cdot \sqrt{3}$ square meters. If its base is $2 \cdot \sqrt{2}$ meters, what is its height with a rationalized denominator?
The length and width of a rectangular canvas are $3 \cdot \sqrt{5}$ meters and $2 \cdot \sqrt{7}$ meters respectively. Find the area of the canvas with a rationalized denominator.
A musician plays a song with a rhythm pattern of $\frac{3}{\sqrt{11}}$ beats per minute. What is this rhythm pattern with a rationalized denominator? Show all steps.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Emphasize the importance of rationalizing denominators in real-world applications.
Tip 2: Encourage students to use the conjugate to rationalize denominators.
Tip 3: Remind students to simplify their answers after rationalizing denominators.
